references.title: A data-driven approach to predict first-year students’ academic success in higher education institutions

extracted.Students: 9652 students for regular BSc, 789 students attending a 4-year BSc program
extracted.Courses: All first-year courses in a Public Portuguese HEI

extracted.Moment of Prediction: Before Start, End of 1st Semester 1st year, End of First Year
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Some of the features were imputted using kNN. Partial time students were excluded.

Authors iterate over different feature combinations andoutlier handling to decide on what is the best approach.
extracted.Models: RPART, DT, CTree, Random Forest, SVM, MLP
extracted.Comments:
Authors use administrative data to predict whether a student will graduate or not within only using data from the first year.

Graduation is understood as graduating in the correct time. Only administrative data is used.

Authors also perform prediction at 3 different stages of the 1st year.
extracted.Limitations:
Single Data Source

Single Institution Data

annotations.text: The main aim of the study is to predict the success of first-year undergraduate students. Authors extract multiple features from both the SIS and LMS as well: socio-demographics, origin, previous qualifications and educational path.
Authors use 3 distinct moments of prediction: before start of year, end of first semester and after second semester, with SVM achieving the best overall performance.
Important features include previous evaluations, study gaps and age-related features when entering college. For later stages, the predictive power is stored on current evaluation features.
